observed_result: Shannon diversity increased with time_months (estimate = 0.0158, p = 0.004246). Group effect and time × group interaction were not significant (group p = 0.7883; interaction p = 0.8301).
interpretation: Alpha diversity appears to increase over follow-up time, but there is no evidence that this trajectory differs between T1D and control groups.
recommended_reporting: Report the full interaction LMM as the primary test and use a reduced time-only LMM as the parsimonious final effect estimate.

observed_result: Bray-Curtis distance from baseline increased with time_from_baseline_months (estimate = 0.0219, p = 9.832e-12). Group effect and time × group interaction were not significant (group p = 0.7326; interaction p = 0.7753).
interpretation: Within-subject community composition progressively diverges from baseline over time, but the degree of divergence is not clearly different between T1D and control groups.
recommended_reporting: Report the full interaction LMM as the primary test and use a reduced time-only baseline-distance LMM as the parsimonious final effect estimate.

observed_result: Subject-level PERMANOVA did not support a group-level community difference (R2 = 0.0270, p = 0.986).
interpretation: After reducing pseudoreplication by averaging samples at the subject level, there is no clear overall group separation.
recommended_reporting: Do not emphasize global T1D-vs-control separation based on beta diversity. Use this result as support for time-dominant longitudinal change.

observed_result: Taxa-level CLR-LMM found 3 FDR-significant time-associated taxa and 0 FDR-significant time × group interaction taxa.
interpretation: Taxa-level interpretation should focus on FDR-significant time effects. Nominal interaction signals should be treated as exploratory only.
recommended_reporting: Report FDR-significant taxa from the full CLR-LMM. Because no interaction taxa pass FDR, avoid group-specific taxon trajectory conclusions.
